creator: Connie Loizos
title: (No AI) Uber’s product chief on hotels, robotaxis, and why the company doesn’t want to be “everything for everyone”
dc:creator: Connie Loizos
content: Uber Chief Product Officer Sachin Kansal walks TechCrunch through the company's financial-services ambitions, its increasingly complicated relationship with Waymo, its new AV Labs data operation, and how AI is starting to show up in ways riders and drivers will actually notice.
contentSnippet: Uber Chief Product Officer Sachin Kansal walks TechCrunch through the company's financial-services ambitions, its increasingly complicated relationship with Waymo, its new AV Labs data operation, and how AI is starting to show up in ways riders and drivers will actually notice.

title: (No AI) Siri AI is already changing how I use my iPhone
author: David Imel
content:
![](https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/07/Siri-AI-Being-invoked-3.jpg?quality=90&strip=all&crop=0,0,100,100)

Siri AI in iOS 27.

iOS 27 escaped the developer world today with the launch of the first public beta. I've been testing the new operating system since early June, looking for quirks and seeing if it can live up to the hype Apple promised in the keynote.

This year's iOS upgrades are what one might call a [Snow Leopard update](https://medium.com/@michaelswengel/macos-27-golden-gate-could-be-apples-next-snow-leopard-if-they-don-t-screw-it-up-c14872a950c8). That means it's light on new features and instead focused on fixing things that were broken and speeding up processes across the OS. App launches, Photos search results, and AirDrop transfers should all be faster. The Messages app now supports in-line replies and end-to-end encryption for RCS messages. Liquid Glass has gotten more refined, …

[Read the full story at The Verge.](https://www.theverge.com/tech/964714/siri-ai-public-beta-preview-ios-27-hands-on)

Liquid Glass has gotten more refined, …

title: (No AI) The 6 wildest claims in Apple’s lawsuit against OpenAI
author: Emma Roth
content:
![A photo of Sam Altman](https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/07/gettyimages-2236544097.jpg?quality=90&strip=all&crop=0,0,100,100)

When Apple employees interviewed for jobs at OpenAI, the AI startup's hardware head allegedly asked them to show up with something unusual: components they were working on and unreleased product samples. That's according to [a blockbuster lawsuit](https://www.documentcloud.org/documents/28453238-complaint-for-trade-secret-misappropriation-and-breach-of-contract/) filed by Apple, which accuses [OpenAI of stealing confidential documents](https://www.theverge.com/tech/964350/apple-openai-lawsuit-trade-secrets), spying on hardware prototypes, and tricking one of its trusted partners into performing a proprietary product design technique.

The lawsuit primarily revolves around the alleged actions of three people:

*   Tang Tan, a 24-year Apple veteran who recently served as the vice president of the Apple Watch. In 2024, Tan left to work on …

[Read the full story at The Verge.](https://www.theverge.com/tech/964843/apple-openai-lawsuit-wildest-claims)

The lawsuit primarily revolves around the alleged actions of three people:
Tang Tan, a 24-year Apple veteran who recently served as the vice president of the Apple Watch. In 2024, Tan left to work on …

Read the full story at The Verge.

creator: Isabella Ward
title: (No AI) Scientists’ Side Hustle? Using AI and Quantum Computing to Generate New Peptides
dc:creator: Isabella Ward
content: Researchers cobbled together funding and time to show how quantum computing could aid in the development of drugs to help underserved populations and combat rare diseases.
